fact: August 3 is exactly 215 days into the year, which means there are exactly 150 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically peak butterfly season - monarchs are often at their most active and colorful right now!
The sun usually rises around 6 AM and sets around 8 PM in most of North America on August 3, giving you about 14 hours of daylight to play outside.
August 3 falls in the middle of Leo season - if the zodiac were a pizza, this date would be right in the cheesiest center slice!
Cicadas are often screaming their loudest on August 3 in many temperate regions - they can buzz at 120 decibels, which is louder than a chainsaw!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, August 3 is deep winter, so kids there are building snowmen while you're sweating in summer!
This date sits exactly 154 days after the spring equinox and 79 days before the fall equinox in the Northern Hemisphere.
Fireflies typically blink their brightest around August 3 in North America - they flash every 6 seconds like tiny living Christmas lights in your backyard!
The Earth is actually moving away from the sun right now - we're about 94.5 million miles away on August 3, compared to 91.4 million miles in early January!
In northern regions like Alaska, the sun barely sets on August 3 - it dips below the horizon for only 4-5 hours, creating an orange-pink sky all night long!
Corn plants are often growing their fastest around August 3 in temperate climates - they can actually grow 2 inches in a single day during peak summer!
The moon phase on August 3 changes every year, but when it's a new moon on this date, you can see about 2,000 more stars than usual because there's no moonlight washing them out!
